description: Give-up timing & confidence-calibration taxonomy across granularity: for every genuine Opus-4.7 meta-off trial (error=None), measure (WHEN) give-up timing on the 13 failures as tool_call_count and turns/max_turns, and (CALIBRATION) classify the final assistant message by lexical markers into ASSERT-SOLVED / DECLINE / OTHER and cross-tabulate against actual solved. No new compute.
motivation: q-0015 asks whether granularity changes WHEN and how confidently the agent gives up, and whether that confidence is calibrated. e-0013 covered engagement depth (calls) and showed wrong-submits=0; q-0015 adds the orthogonal axes of give-up TIMING (fraction of turn budget; voluntary vs budget-exhaustion) and CONFIDENCE CALIBRATION (does the agent ever overclaim a solve it did not achieve?).
formal_results: Per failed trial: tool_call_count, assistant-turns, turns/max_turns. Per trial: final-message confidence class in {ASSERT-SOLVED, DECLINE, OTHER} x solved. Aggregates: overclaim count, honest-decline count, success-assertion precision, decline precision, #failures hitting max_turns.
results_description: 62 genuine trials (49 solved, 13 failed). WHEN (failed, mean turns/budget): none 2.0% (1.0 calls), coarse 5.0% (3.0), scratchpad 26.0% (31.5), fine 25.3% (43.3), medium 54.8% (55.7). none 2% vs tools 30.2% of turn budget. Only 1/13 failures hit max_turns (one medium); the other 12 are voluntary early declines. CALIBRATION: ASSERT-SOLVED on 31 trials, ALL solved (0 failed) -> success-claim precision 31/31=100%. DECLINE on exactly the 13 failures, 0 solves -> decline precision 13/13=100%. OVERCLAIM (failed but asserts solved)=0/13; honest-decline=13/13; wrong-submits=0/13 (corroborates e-0013). Per-toolset: every toolset declines on 100% of its failures, 0 overclaims.
conclusions: Granularity strongly moves give-up TIMING but NOT calibration. Finer tools abandon much later: none/coarse decline almost immediately (2-5% of the turn budget, 1-3 calls) while fine/medium/scratchpad grind 25-55% of the budget first - yet abandonment is VOLUNTARY (only 1/13 failures is budget-forced), so the later stop reflects deeper self-directed search, not running out of room. Confidence is perfectly calibrated and granularity-INVARIANT: the 'I solved it' assertion never appears on a failure (precision 100%) and the explicit decline never appears on a solve (precision 100%); 0 overclaims and 0 wrong-submits across all toolsets. So coarser tooling changes WHEN the agent gives up but not the honesty or calibration of the give-up. Complements a-0016/e-0013 (effort inflation, early-refuse vs grind-then-refuse): tools buy a longer voluntary search whose stopping decision stays perfectly truthful.
